concepts.name: Daily Fill
concepts.description: A fill providing sufficient supply for one day

concepts.name: Emergency Supply
concepts.description: A supply action where there is no 'valid' order for the supplied medication. E.g. Emergency vacation supply, weekend supply (when prescriber is unavailable to provide a renewal prescription)

concepts.name: First Fill
concepts.description: The initial fill against an order. (This includes initial fills against refill orders.)

concepts.name: First Fill - Complete
concepts.description: A first fill where the quantity supplied is equal to one full repetition of the ordered amount. (e.g. If the order was 90 tablets, 3 refills, a complete fill would be for the full 90 tablets).

concepts.name: First Fill - Part Fill
concepts.description: A first fill where the quantity supplied is less than one full repetition of the ordered amount. (e.g. If the order was 90 tablets, 3 refills, a partial fill might be for only 30 tablets.)

concepts.name: first fill, partial strength
concepts.description: A first fill where the strength supplied is less than the ordered strength. (e.g. 10mg for an order of 50mg where a subsequent fill will dispense 40mg tablets).

concepts.name: Floor stock
concepts.description: A supply action to restock a smaller more local dispensary.

concepts.name: Manufacturer Sample
concepts.description: A supply of a manufacturer sample

concepts.name: Refill
concepts.description: A fill against an order that has already been filled (or partially filled) at least once.

concepts.name: Refill - Complete
concepts.description: A refill where the quantity supplied is equal to one full repetition of the ordered amount. (e.g. If the order was 90 tablets, 3 refills, a complete fill would be for the full 90 tablets.)

concepts.name: refill complete partial strength
concepts.description: A refill where the quantity supplied is equal to one full repetition of the ordered amount. (e.g. If the order was 90 tablets, 3 refills, a complete fill would be for the full 90 tablets.) and where the strength supplied is less than the ordered strength (e.g. 10mg for an order of 50mg where a subsequent fill will dispense 40mg tablets).

concepts.name: Refill (First fill this facility)
concepts.description: The first fill against an order that has already been filled at least once at another facility.

concepts.name: refill partial strength (first fill this facility)
concepts.enDisplayName: refill partial strength (first fill this facility)
concepts.description: The first fill against an order that has already been filled at least once at another facility and where the strength supplied is less than the ordered strength (e.g. 10mg for an order of 50mg where a subsequent fill will dispense 40mg tablets).

concepts.name: Refill - Part Fill
concepts.description: A refill where the quantity supplied is less than one full repetition of the ordered amount. (e.g. If the order was 90 tablets, 3 refills, a partial fill might be for only 30 tablets.)

concepts.name: refill part fill partial strength
concepts.description: A refill where the quantity supplied is less than one full repetition of the ordered amount. (e.g. If the order was 90 tablets, 3 refills, a partial fill might be for only 30 tablets.) and where the strength supplied is less than the ordered strength (e.g. 10mg for an order of 50mg where a subsequent fill will dispense 40mg tablets).

concepts.name: refill partial strength
concepts.description: A fill against an order that has already been filled (or partially filled) at least once and where the strength supplied is less than the ordered strength (e.g. 10mg for an order of 50mg where a subsequent fill will dispense 40mg tablets).

concepts.name: Script Owing
concepts.description: An emergency supply where the expectation is that a formal order authorizing the supply will be provided at a later date.

concepts.name: Trial Balance
concepts.description: A fill where the remainder of a 'complete' fill is provided after a trial fill has been provided.

concepts.name: trial balance partial strength
concepts.frDisplayName: Restant de qté d'essai - concentration partielle
concepts.description: A fill where the remainder of a 'complete' fill is provided after a trial fill has been provided and where the strength supplied is less than the ordered strength (e.g. 10mg for an order of 50mg where a subsequent fill will dispense 40mg tablets).

concepts.name: Trial Fill
concepts.description: A fill where a small portion is provided to allow for determination of the therapy effectiveness and patient tolerance.

concepts.name: Unit Dose
concepts.description: A supply action that provides sufficient material for a single dose.

concepts.name: unit dose equivalent
concepts.description: A supply action that provides sufficient material for a single dose via multiple products. E.g. 2 50mg tablets for a 100mg unit dose.
